message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
summary: Adding trans_metadata (note/created/modified) parsing to InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in ext.py

type: CLAIM
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
summary: Adding domain parameter support to trans tag in InternationalizationExtension.parse/_make_node/_install_callables

type: STATUS
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
summary: Done: added _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS (note/created/modered) handling in parse() before the assign/trimmed checks, added metadata dict param as new trailing arg to _make_node() which sets output_node.trans_metadata when non-empty. Also fixed comma-expectation check from 'if variables:' to 'if variables or metadata:' at top of parse loop so consecutive metadata params require commas like PR examples show. If your domain param also adds a new _make_node param, watch for signature/positional-arg conflicts when merging.

type: STATUS
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
summary: Done: added domain param parsing in parse() (checked via token.value == 'domain' inside the existing assign branch, no new state added to variables dict, so I did NOT touch the 'if variables:' comma-expectation line), added domain arg to _make_node() (new positional param after num_called_num, generates dgettext/dngettext calls), added _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext factories and dgettext/dngettext plumbing through _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall. If our patches get merged, watch for: _make_node() now has domain as an added trailing positional param (same position style as your metadata dict) and the parse() loop's assign-branch now special-cases token.value=='domain' before the generic variables[...] assignment.
